description:
attached_molecule: gt-wisp-nfwh
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-06T11:56:28Z
dispatched_by: gastown/crew/scout

Observed after gt-bzo/gt-a5a/gt-9ng completion:
- `bd show` for all three source issues is closed
- `gt hook show <rig>/polecats/<name>` returns status=empty
- but `gt polecat list gastown --json` still reports state=working and session_running=true for furiosa/nux/slit

This makes coordinator status checks noisy and can cause false 'still working' assumptions.

Acceptance:
- After successful `gt done`, polecat state transitions to idle (or equivalent non-working state)
- `gt polecat list` and `gt hook show` agree on completion state
title: Polecat state remains 'working' with empty hook after gt done

description:
attached_molecule: gt-wisp-0fmm
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-06T11:56:10Z
dispatched_by: gastown/crew/scout

Build `gt witness check [rig] --json` to run witness health checks in Go instead of formula prose.

Scope:
- Resolve correct refinery session name from rig prefix (no raw tmux-name guessing)
- Report refinery running state
- Report deacon running state with grace-window metadata
- Query queue health from refinery APIs (`gt refinery ready --all --json` equivalent data)
- Emit machine-readable JSON suitable for formula use

Acceptance:
- Witness formula step `check-refinery` can be reduced to a single command call
- Unit tests cover prefix resolution + JSON schema
- No direct shell-out to `tmux has-session <full-rig-name>-refinery` in formula path
notes: Rebased on latest origin/main after MR conflict. Resolved mol-witness-patrol.formula.toml conflict by preserving single-command check-refinery step based on gt witness check --json. Re-ran go test ./internal/cmd successfully before resubmission.
title: Add gt witness check compiled health command

description: ore_v3_master had long-stuck merge queue entries (ovm-efyl and peers). Manual push of master unblocked git state, but MR beads remained open and queue did not self-drain. I manually cherry-picked branch tips to /home/henry/gt/ore_v3_master/refinery/rig master, pushed origin/master through 94a5322, then manually closed ovm-usts ovm-sp3s ovm-geno ovm-5c0s8 to clear queue. Expected: refinery detects branch commit already in master, auto-closes corresponding MR beads, and advances queue without manual bead closure.
title: Merge queue does not auto-close already-merged MR beads

close_reason: Refiling in auditor repo per user direction
description: Observed on 2026-02-21 during manual backlog drain. Same hooked issue was active on multiple workers simultaneously: wjs-wisp-bwn on wormhole_just_solana/chrome and wormhole_just_solana/rust; sss-wisp-ati6 on solana_system_stake/rictus and solana_system_stake/slit; sl-wisp-82j on solana_loaders/obsidian and solana_loaders/quartz. This caused duplicate execution risk and required manual nudges + assignee correction. Expected: strict single-assignee lock per bead with automatic duplicate-run prevention.

description: While re-dispatching backlog intake beads, gt sling failed with: bead <id> already has attached molecule(s). Example errors: sl-wisp-82j had sl-wisp-dixxf + sl-wisp-hhpkm attached; sss-wisp-ati6 had sss-wisp-c58o attached. Command failed after spawning a new polecat, leaving extra sessions. Recovery required gt sling --force which burns stale molecules. Expected: non-destructive stale-molecule recovery path or automatic rebind to existing active molecule.
title: Sling cannot recover stale attached molecules without --force burn

description:
attached_molecule: gt-wisp-rdyc
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-06T10:43:48Z
dispatched_by: mayor

After fresh HQ beads DB initialization, 'gt compact report' fails with: 'all sends failed: deacon/: invalid recipient "deacon/": no agent found'. The HQ beads db had issue_prefix missing (fixed manually via 'bd config set issue_prefix hq'), but the agent bead for deacon/ was never seeded. The compact report tries to send mail to 'deacon/' which doesn't exist as an agent bead in the fresh HQ database. Needs: either (1) seed agent beads during HQ init, or (2) make gt compact report tolerate missing agent beads gracefully.
notes: Implemented fix on branch polecat/slit/gt-9ng@mmero5q9: removed stale deacon/ recipient references from compact report docs/comments and added regression test TestSendCompactDigestSendsToMayorOnly to enforce mayor-only target. Validation: go test ./internal/cmd (full package) passed.
title: gt compact report fails: deacon/ agent not found in fresh HQ db

notes: Reproduced with: bd cook mol-orphan-scan -> vars.scope cannot have both required:true and default. Fixed internal/formula/formulas/mol-orphan-scan.formula.toml by removing required=true from vars.scope while keeping default='town'. Verification: bd cook internal/formula/formulas/mol-orphan-scan.formula.toml succeeds; go test ./internal/formula/... and go test ./internal/doctor/... pass.
title: mol-orphan-scan formula broken: vars.scope has both required:true and default, causing validation failure on gt sling

close_reason: Fixed by gt doctor --fix which created 2 missing agent beads. Root cause: agent beads weren't registered in mail system. Now resolved.
description: gt mail send deacon/ and gt mail send mayor/ both fail with 'Error: all sends failed: <identity>/: invalid recipient: no agent found'. This breaks: 1) gt compact report (tries to send to deacon/), 2) Deacon lifecycle messaging, 3) cross-agent notifications. The agent beads for deacon and mayor are not registered in the mail system. Need to investigate how agent beads get created and why they're missing for hq-deacon and hq-mayor.
title: gt mail: deacon/ and mayor/ fail with 'no agent found'

description:
attached_molecule: gt-wisp-v9o9
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-06T10:42:34Z
dispatched_by: mayor

When a witness completes a patrol cycle, gt patrol report closes the current wisp and creates a new one. But the wisps.hook_bead field on the agent wisp is set during attach and never cleared by gt unsling or gt mol detach. When a wisp is reaped (gc'd), the witness's hook still points to the reaped wisp ID. On next patrol cycle, the witness tries to bd show the reaped wisp, gets 'not found', and enters an escalation loop.

Fix: gt unsling (or wherever the patrol cycle closes/transitions) should clear the hook_bead field on the agent's wisp row. Also: gt prime/gt hook should handle 'hooked wisp not found' gracefully instead of propagating the error.

Reproduction: Run witness patrol for several cycles. After wisp GC runs and reaps old wisps, the witness will get stuck trying to show reaped wisp IDs.

Related: handoff notes mention this was manually SQL-fixed 3 times. The field is legacy (intentional no-op per hq-l6mm5) but prime_session still reads it.
notes:
Implemented hook_bead stale-pointer fix across unsling/patrol/prime paths.

Changes:
- Added agent hook metadata helpers that set/clear both description field and slot (slot set/clear + description update).
- gt unsling now clears agent hook metadata when unhooking and also self-heals when no hooked bead is found.
- gt patrol report clears previous patrol hook metadata; autoSpawnPatrol now sets hook metadata to the newly created patrol wisp.
- gt prime, gt prime --state detection, and gt hook/mol status now treat hook_bead-not-found as recoverable: they clear stale pointer and fall back to assignee lookup.
- Added HookBead support to beads.AgentFieldUpdates so hook metadata can be updated atomically.

Validation:
- go test ./internal/cmd ./internal/beads
title: gt unsling does not clear wisps.hook_bead field, causing witnesses to get stuck on reaped wisps

description:
attached_molecule: gt-wisp-p10a
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-06T11:56:15Z
dispatched_by: gastown/crew/scout

Deacon is crashing roughly every 15 minutes while running `mol-deacon-patrol` (~1171 lines) on haiku.

Goal:
Pick and implement the fastest stable path:
1) Trim formula/token load enough to run reliably on haiku, OR
2) Upgrade deacon model in town settings to a stronger model (with explicit rollback path)

Deliverables:
- Measured before/after deacon uptime over at least 2 heartbeat windows
- Documented model/formula choice and rationale
- Updated operational notes for deacon restart + recovery workflow

Acceptance:
- No deacon death/restart loop during observation window
- Deacon continues executing real patrol cycles (not synthetic heartbeat loops)
notes:
Stabilization path selected: stronger model plus startup hardening.

Model and runtime choice with rationale:
- Deacon was restarted at 2026-03-06T12:53:19Z with agent override claude (Opus-class) to avoid haiku context-limit collapse during mol-deacon-patrol.
- Startup hardening was added in internal/cmd/deacon.go to match manager startup behavior: remain-on-exit enabled immediately, runtime liveness environment merged, and tmux auto-respawn hook set.

Measured before and after over at least two heartbeat windows:
- BEFORE window: 2026-03-06T12:11:35Z to 2026-03-06T12:17:52Z (6m17s)
  - deacon session_start events: 7
- AFTER window: 2026-03-06T12:53:19Z to 2026-03-06T12:59:39Z (6m20s)
  - deacon session_start events: 1 (initial start only)
  - additional restarts after initial start: 0
  - deacon-related escalation_sent events: 0

Real patrol-cycle confirmation (not synthetic heartbeat loop):
- Live deacon terminal during observation showed actual formula progression, including cycle close via patrol report and transition into subsequent cycles (for example cycle 4 to 5 to 7), with normal patrol commands executed inside steps (dog status, rig list, gate checks).
title: Stabilize deacon patrol loop under haiku context limits

description: When slinging to a rig (e.g. auditor), gt sling looks for mol-polecat-work in auditor/mayor/rig/ and fails if not found. It should fall back to the central formula store (gastown/.beads/formulas/). Formulas are shared infrastructure, not per-rig artifacts. Currently requires --hook-raw-bead workaround which skips lifecycle instructions entirely.
title: gt sling formula resolution should fall back to central GT formulas, not only look in target rig

close_reason: Fixed: handoff now uses role_agents resolution
description:
dispatched_by: mayor

## Bug

In `internal/cmd/handoff.go`, `buildRestartCommand()` at line 541 calls:

```go
runtimeCmd = config.GetRuntimeCommandWithPrompt(rigPath, beacon)
```

This uses `ResolveAgentConfig()` which resolves the **default** agent, ignoring the `role_agents` map in town/rig settings. So when a witness (configured as `claude-haiku` via `role_agents.witness`) does a self-handoff, it restarts as the default agent (opus) instead of haiku.

## Expected

Handoff should preserve the role-based agent config. The witness manager's `buildWitnessStartCommand()` correctly uses `BuildAgentStartupCommandWithAgentOverride()` which calls `ResolveRoleAgentConfig()` — the handoff path should do the same.

## Fix

In `buildRestartCommand()`, replace the call to `GetRuntimeCommandWithPrompt()` with role-aware resolution, e.g.:

```go
simpleRole := config.ExtractSimpleRole(gtRole)
runtimeCmd, err = config.BuildAgentStartupCommand(simpleRole, identity.Rig, townRoot, rigPath, beacon)
```

This ensures handoff respects `role_agents` from `settings/config.json`.

## Key files
- `internal/cmd/handoff.go` line ~541 (buildRestartCommand)
- `internal/config/loader.go` (ResolveRoleAgentConfig vs ResolveAgentConfig)
- `internal/witness/manager.go` line ~234 (correct pattern to follow)
- `~/gt/settings/config.json` (role_agents config)

Create a PR with the fix.
title: handoff.go buildRestartCommand uses GetRuntimeCommandWithPrompt which ignores role_agents config, causing self-handoff to lose model flag
